description: setup_payment_dev_buffers checks the cudaError_t after every CUDA call and cleans up the half-allocated state on failure; the other two siblings batch errors at the end via cudaGetLastError. Unifying onto a shared setup() that uses only the batched check re-introduces the silent-corrupt-memory bug from the payment-OOM incident.

findings.rationale: setup_payment_dev_buffers has BRANCHes after each CUDA call with explicit cudaFree cleanup; the other two have no BRANCHes and just call the final cudaGetLastError. Significant structural divergence — multiple extra BRANCHes, multiple extra cleanup CALLs.
